signals.raw: XRP Breaks Cup-and-Handle Pattern With Record ETF Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**

signals.headline: XRP has surged 12
signals.raw:
XRP has surged 12.5% from its April lows** as U.S. spot ETFs recorded $41.64 million in net inflows for the week ending April 16—the largest single-week inflow in three months[1]. This represents a 254% jump from the prior week's $11.75 million, signaling acceleration that "typically accompanies pattern breakouts rather than consolidation."[1]

The technical confirmation: XRP's cup-and-handle patt

signals.headline: Market catalysts**: The CLARITY Act markup is scheduled for late April 2026, which aims to provide permanent regulatory
signals.raw: Market catalysts**: The CLARITY Act markup is scheduled for late April 2026, which aims to provide permanent regulatory status for digital assets[4]. The SEC vs. Ripple lawsuit concluded in 2025, and new institutional integrations—including Rakuten Wallet in Japan—are fueling demand[4].

signals.headline: Based on the search results, here's the breaking news:

signals.headline: CAVALIERS TAKE COMMANDING 2-0 SERIES LEAD WITH DOMINANT GAME 2 WIN | CAVALIERS FAVORED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
CAVALIERS TAKE COMMANDING 2-0 SERIES LEAD WITH DOMINANT GAME 2 WIN | CAVALIERS FAVORED | HIGH confidence**

The **Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 115-105** in Game 2 on Monday night to take a **2-0 lead** in their first-round Eastern Conference series[1][2]. This positions Cleveland as heavy favorites to advance.
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: **Star power overwhelms Toronto:** Donovan Mitchell (30 points), James Harden (28 points), and Evan Mobley (25 points) c
signals.raw: **Star power overwhelms Toronto:** Donovan Mitchell (30 points), James Harden (28 points), and Evan Mobley (25 points) combined for 83 points, with Mitchell and Harden accounting for 68% of Cleveland's scoring[2].

signals.headline: **Historic dominance:** The Cavaliers have now won **12 consecutive playoff games against Toronto**, tying the NBA recor
signals.raw: **Historic dominance:** The Cavaliers have now won **12 consecutive playoff games against Toronto**, tying the NBA record for consecutive wins against a single opponent in the postseason[2][3].
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: **Toronto's struggle:** Scottie Barnes scored a playoff career-high 26 points, but Brandon Ingram severely underperforme
signals.raw: **Toronto's struggle:** Scottie Barnes scored a playoff career-high 26 points, but Brandon Ingram severely underperformed with just 7 points on 3-for-15 shooting[2].

signals.headline: **Series momentum:** Cleveland never trailed in Game 2, extending their control heading into Game 3 in Toronto on Thursd
signals.raw:
**Series momentum:** Cleveland never trailed in Game 2, extending their control heading into Game 3 in Toronto on Thursday night[2].

The Cavaliers' balanced offensive firepower and Toronto's inability to get role players like Ingram going strongly favor a Cleveland series victory.
